title: Research & development spending as a share of GDP
subtitle: Includes basic research, applied research, and experimental development.
note: Spending includes current and capital expenditures (public and private) on research.
caption:
Research & development spending as a share of GDP

Includes basic research, applied research, and experimental development.

This choropleth world map displays 2023 research and development spending as a share of GDP, covering basic research, applied research, and experimental development. The legend ranges from 0% to over 4%, with specific tick marks at 1%, 2%, 3%, and 4%, alongside a hatched pattern indicating no data. Spending includes current and capital expenditures from both public and private sectors. The darkest green band, representing values above 4%, is occupied by a small cluster of nations including South Korea, Japan, and Israel. The United States and several countries in Northern and Western Europe, such as Sweden, Finland, and Germany, appear in the next darkest band, falling between the 3% and 4% thresholds. China and Russia sit in a mid-range green shade that corresponds to the 2% to 3% interval. A broad group of countries, including Australia, Brazil, Canada, and India, are colored in lighter shades of green that place them in the 1% to 2% range. Much of Africa, the Middle East, and parts of South America appear in the lightest visible shade, indicating shares below 1%. Large portions of the African continent, along with Greenland and several island nations, display a diagonal hatching pattern, signifying that no data is available for these regions. The visual distribution shows the highest spending shares concentrated in East Asia, parts of Europe, and North America, while lower shares and missing data characterize much of the Global South.
